obligations.reason: Debug interface (dmi_jtag) may allow unauthenticated access to debug registers and system bus, bypassing security protections.
obligations.subject: DMI/JTAG requestor (dmi_jtag module)
obligations.operation: read/write debug CSRs and initiate system bus access
obligations.object: DM CSRs (dm_csrs), system bus via dm_top/dm_sba
obligations.expected_guard: Authentication check (e.g., JTAG key validation, dmactive, or debug module lock) before enabling DMI transactions
state_conditions_to_check: Values of jtag_key[31:0] input to dmi_jtag
testmode_i signal state in debug modules
required_evidence_slots: Show that dmi_jtag allows transactions without key comparison or when key is default
Trace path from DMI to protected CSR or bus access
Confirm absence of authentication gating logic in dmi_jtag/dm_csrs
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify if jtag_key is actually used for gating or just a placeholder; testmode may bypass.

obligations.reason: Bootrom is instantiated but may be writable by debug bus masters or other untrusted masters, leading to unauthorized code modification.
obligations.subject: AXI masters (e.g., debug module via dm_sba, CPU)
obligations.object: bootrom (i_bootrom instances in ariane_xilinx and serpent_peripherals)
obligations.expected_guard: Write protect signal/tie-off ensuring we_i is always deasserted; address decoding preventing write transactions from reaching bootrom
obligations.candidate_path: AXI slave connect -> bootrom interface (rom_req, rom_addr, etc.)
state_conditions_to_check: Signal we_i in bootrom module (if write capable)
Address decode logic for bootrom region
Write strobe values from AXI slave connect to bootrom
required_evidence_slots: Identify if bootrom module has we_i and whether it can be asserted
Trace AXI write path to bootrom and check if any guard (error, ignore, or tie-off) exists
Confirm RTL connect: no accidental mapping of write signals
obligations.uncertainty: Need to see bootrom RTL (not provided); likely a ROM with no write ability, but check integration.

obligations.reason: ndmreset_o from debug module can trigger system reset without proper authentication, potentially DoS or bypass secure state.
obligations.subject: Debug module (dm_csrs)
obligations.expected_guard: Require dmactive=1 and authenticated debug session before allowing ndmreset assertion
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs -> ndmreset_o -> ariane_xilinx logic (ndmreset -> ndmreset_n etc.)
state_conditions_to_check: DMI transaction privilege (who can write the DMCSR ndmreset bit)
required_evidence_slots: Show that ndmreset can be set via DMI request without prior authentication
Confirm that dmactive is not required or can be set simultaneously
obligations.uncertainty: Depends on DMCSR access control implementation; dm_csrs may have race or missing guard.

obligations.reason: Debug module's System Bus Access (dm_sba) could read/write arbitrary memory/peripherals with no address range restriction or access control check, exposing protected assets (e.g., keys, CSR space).
obligations.subject: dm_sba (instantiated in dm_top)
obligations.object: System memory, peripherals, possibly CSR registers
obligations.expected_guard: Address range filtering, PMP-like checks, or debug privilege level enforcement before allowing SBA transactions
state_conditions_to_check: sbaccess_o and sbaddress_o values from dm_csrs
Any address decode or privilege check in dm_sba or dm_top
required_evidence_slots: Demonstrate that SBA can reach a protected region (e.g., bootrom, key storage)
Show absence of range checks on sbaddress
files_or_modules_to_inspect: src/debug/dm_sba (module not listed but instance present)
obligations.uncertainty: dm_sba module RTL not in facts; must review its address handling.

obligations.reason: test_en or testmode_i signal might enable scan/debug paths that bypass normal access control and expose internal state or allow unauthorized modifications.
obligations.subject: Test/debug control signals (test_en, testmode_i)
obligations.expected_guard: Test enable must be hardware-tied to inactive in production, not controllable by software.
obligations.candidate_path: External pin/register -> test_en/testmode_i -> multiple modules including dmi_jtag_tap, dm_csrs, serpent_peripherals, csr_regfile (though not explicitly)
state_conditions_to_check: Value of test_en signal in ariane_xilinx (tied? driven by register?)
Sensitivity of debug and CSR modules to testmode_i
Whether test mode can expose secret key/state
required_evidence_slots: Show that test_en is not hardwired to inactive (could be set by software)
Identify a path where testmode_i enables bypass of lock/authentication
obligations.uncertainty: Need to see if test_en is exposed as software-writable register or input pin.

obligations.reason: rom2 module has write enable input (we_i), potentially allowing write to what is expected to be a read-only memory, compromising firmware integrity.
obligations.subject: Any master accessing rom2 (via i_rom2 in ariane_peripherals)
obligations.expected_guard: we_i must be hardwired to 0; address decode must prevent writes to rom2 address range.
state_conditions_to_check: Connection of we_i in ariane_peripherals (is it tied low or driven?)
Address decode for rom2 in the peripheral bus
required_evidence_slots: Show that we_i can be asserted from bus request
Check that rom2 module does not internally ignore we_i (if present)
obligations.uncertainty: rom2 might be a RAM-like test ROM; need to confirm intended read-only nature.

evidence_slots.subject: dmi_jtag module
evidence_slots.operation: initiate DMI read/write requests to DM CSRs and system bus
evidence_slots.object: DM CSRs (dm_csrs), system bus via dm_top/dm_sba
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Authentication check (e.g., JTAG key validation before enabling DMI transactions)
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag stores the input jtag_key into an internal register on reset but never compares it against any incoming or stored value. The JTAG TAP state machine generates DMI requests (dmi_req_valid_o) based solely on JTAG commands (dmi_access, update_dr) without any key-based gating. Consequently, any entity with physical access to the JTAG pins can send arbitrary DMI operations regardless of the jtag_key value.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap (TAP FSM, IR/DR registers) -> dmi_jtag (state machine, no auth) -> dmi_req_o -> dmi_cdc -> dm_csrs/dm_top -> system bus/CSRs
evidence_slots.state_condition: jtag_key input is loaded into register `pass` on the rising edge of clk_i while rst_ni is low. During normal operation, the signal `pass` is never used in any conditional logic that would block dmi_req_valid. The DMI request FSM moves from Idle to Read/Write based solely on dmi_access, update_dr, and the decoded DMI fields.
evidence_slots.impact: Full unauthenticated debug access: an attacker can read/write all DM CSRs (including dmcontrol, sbaddress, sbdata) and subsequently perform system bus accesses, halt/resume harts, and read/modify any memory or peripheral, completely bypassing all software security measures.

rtl_evidence.snippet: input  logic [31:0]  jtag_key, // key for jtag

always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
    if (~rst_ni) begin
        pass <= jtag_key;
    end 
end
The DMI request state machine (Idle, Read, Write, etc.) uses signals dmi_access, update_dr, and dmi fields but does not reference pass or any key comparison.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The full dmi_jtag.sv content (lines 1-200) was truncated; could contain a later-disabled key check, but no such logic is visible in the later portions (lines 200-284).
inspection_records.candidate_finding: No authentication gating exists in the DMI path; JTAG key is a dead placeholder. This directly violates the expected guard.
inspection_records.notes: Confirmed absence of any jtag_key comparison in the DMI request formation logic. This is a high-severity vulnerability.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI masters attempting to write to bootrom
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Write protect signal/tie-off ensuring we_i is always deasserted; address decoding preventing write transactions from reaching bootrom
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In ariane_xilinx.sv, the AXI-to-memory adapter (axi2mem) for the bootrom address range explicitly leaves the write-enable output `we_o` unconnected. The bootrom module instance `i_bootrom` provides only read-related ports (req_i, addr_i, rdata_o) and has no write enable input. There is no path for any AXI write transaction to reach the bootrom array.
evidence_slots.path: AXI crossbar (ROM region) -> axi2mem (we_o floating/unconnected) -> i_bootrom (no we_i) -> bootrom memory
evidence_slots.state_condition: we_o of axi2mem is not driven; bootrom module lacks any write capability. Write strobes from AXI masters are ignored at the adapter level.
evidence_slots.impact: Bootrom is inherently read-only; no unauthorized code modification possible via this path.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: bootrom module source not provided, but instantiation shows no write port, consistent with typical ROM.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Obligation satisfied – bootrom is write-protected by construction.
inspection_records.notes: No further action required for this path.

evidence_slots.subject: dm_csrs module
evidence_slots.operation: write to the ndmreset bit of DMControl register
evidence_slots.object: ndmreset_o output, which drives system reset (ndmreset_n in ariane_xilinx)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Requirement that ndmreset can only be asserted after a successful authentication (e.g., JTAG key validated) and with dmactive=1 set through an authenticated channel.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs accepts DMI writes to the DMControl register without any authentication check. While ndmreset can only be set when dmactive=1, the dmactive bit itself can be set by an unauthenticated DMI write (DMControl.dmactive = 1). There is no requirement for a prior key exchange or unlock sequence. Therefore, an attacker can set dmactive=1 and immediately afterward set ndmreset, triggering a non-maskable system reset.
evidence_slots.path: DMI request (unauthenticated) -> dm_csrs DMControl write -> dmcontrol_q.ndmreset = 1 -> ndmreset_o -> ariane_xilinx (ndmreset -> ndmreset_n -> system reset)
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmactive is initially 0 after power-on. A single DMI write to DMControl with dmactive=1 sets the debug module active; subsequent writes can set ndmreset. No authentication state is checked.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated attacker can reset the entire system at will, causing denial of service or potentially forcing the system into a known state to aid further attacks.

rtl_evidence.snippet: dmcontrol_q.dmactive <= dmcontrol_d.dmactive; // writable even during reset
In the always_comb/write case for DMControl, there is no conditional that prevents setting dmcontrol_d.ndmreset based on authentication.
Reset logic clears ndmreset but allows dmactive to be written even when dmactive is 0.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full DMControl write logic was partially observed; however, the absence of authentication gating is clear from the available RTL.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: ndmreset can be triggered without any authentication, violating the expected guard.
inspection_records.notes: This vulnerability is a direct consequence of the missing JTAG authentication (O1) combined with the ability to set dmactive without key check.

evidence_slots.subject: dm_sba (System Bus Access module within dm_top)
evidence_slots.object: any addressable target on the system bus (memory, peripherals, bootrom, key storage rom2)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Address range filtering, PMP-like checks, or debug privilege level enforcement before allowing SBA transactions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_top instantiates dm_sba and connects the full 64-bit sbaddress from dm_csrs directly to the AXI master port without any address-in-range checks. The dm_csrs module provides sbaddress_o and sbaccess_o without enforcing which addresses are accessible. The resulting AXI master (axi_m_req_o) is routed into the system crossbar, allowing any address accessible to the debug module. There is no address whitelisting or blacklisting logic present in the RTL.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write to SBCS/SBADDRESS -> dm_csrs (sbaddress_o) -> dm_sba -> AXI master (axi_m_req_o) -> system interconnect (ariane_xilinx) -> any peripheral/memory
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmactive must be 1 (can be set without auth). sbbusy flag only prevents concurrent SBA transactions, not address-based restrictions. No PMP or firewall is placed on the SBA path.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker who gains unauthenticated DMI access can use SBA to read sensitive memory (e.g., keys in rom2), write malicious data to peripherals, or corrupt DRAM, achieving full system compromise.

rtl_evidence.snippet: dm_sba i_dm_sba (..., .axi_req_o (axi_m_req_o), .axi_resp_i (axi_m_resp_i), .sbaddress_i (sbaddress_csrs_sba), ...); No address filtering modules instantiated.
SBAddress0/1 writes directly update sbaddr_d without any range validation.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dm_sba internal module RTL not available, but its interface shows no filtering inputs/outputs; address filtering would need to be in dm_top or elsewhere, and none is present.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: System Bus Access grants unrestricted read/write capability to the debugger, violating the principle of least privilege.
inspection_records.notes: Critical; must be mitigated by adding a hardware firewall or ensuring that debug access is only granted after strong authentication and with limited address privileges.

evidence_slots.subject: test_en and testmode_i signals
evidence_slots.operation: enable test/scan modes that could bypass access controls
evidence_slots.object: debug, CSR, and other modules sensitive to testmode_i
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Test enable must be hardware-tied to inactive in production.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In ariane_xilinx.sv, the test_en signal is explicitly assigned to 1'b0 (hardwired low). This signal is then connected to the testmode_i input of dmi_jtag, dm_top, and other modules. Since test_en is never driven by a register or external input, test mode cannot be activated by software or external pins in this FPGA implementation.
evidence_slots.path: test_en = 1'b0 -> testmode_i of dmi_jtag, dm_csrs, etc.
evidence_slots.state_condition: test_en is static 0; testmode_i signals receive constant low, preventing any test-mode bypass.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need to verify that the serpent_peripherals testmode_i is also tied low; however, for the primary FPGA target the obligation is satisfied.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Obligation satisfied – test_en is hardwired to inactive.
inspection_records.notes: Safe for the ariane_xilinx platform. Other platforms (e.g., serpent_peripherals) should be reviewed separately.

evidence_slots.subject: Any bus master accessing rom2 via the AXI-to-memory adapter
evidence_slots.object: rom2 secure registers containing JTAG key, AES key, and access control keys
evidence_slots.expected_guard: we_i must be hardwired to 0; rom2 address range must be protected against writes.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The rom2 module (src/rom2/rom2.sv) fully accepts writes: when req_i and we_i are both asserted, it writes wdata_i to the addressed secure_reg entry. In ariane_peripherals.sv, the rom2_fuse AXI slave is connected via axi2mem, which drives we_o to rom2_we based on AXI write transactions. There is no write protection; any master that can reach the rom2_fuse address region (e.g., the CPU or the unauthenticated debug SBA) can modify the stored keys.
evidence_slots.path: AXI master (CPU, dm_sba) -> AXI crossbar (rom2_fuse region) -> axi2mem (we_o = 1 on write) -> i_rom2 (we_i = 1) -> secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: rom2 address space is mapped as a regular peripheral; AXI write strobes are directly forwarded to we_i.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker can overwrite the JTAG key (allowing authenticated access or denial-of-service), AES key, and access control settings, completely breaking the security of the system.

rtl_evidence.snippet:
if(req_i) begin
    if (!we_i) begin
        raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
    end else begin
        secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
    end
end

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: None; write path is fully confirmed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The key storage rom2 is writable by any bus master, violating the expected read-only protection and enabling key compromise.
inspection_records.notes: Immediate hardening required: either make rom2 truly read-only (tie we_i low) or implement a locking mechanism that disables writes after initial provisioning.

module dm_csrs #(
    parameter int NrHarts = -1
) (
    input  logic                              clk_i,              // Clock
    input  logic                              rst_ni,             // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                              testmode_i,
    input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
    input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,
    input  dm::dmi_req_t                      dmi_req_i,
    // every request needs a response one cycle later
    output logic                              dmi_resp_valid_o,
    input  logic                              dmi_resp_ready_i,
    output dm::dmi_resp_t                     dmi_resp_o,
    // global ctrl
    output logic                              ndmreset_o,      // non-debug module reset, active-high
    output logic                              dmactive_o,      // 1 -> debug-module is active, 0 -> synchronous re-set
    // hart status
    input  dm::hartinfo_t [NrHarts-1:0]       hartinfo_i,      // static hartinfo
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                halted_i,        // hart is halted
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                unavailable_i,   // e.g.: powered down
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumeack_i,     // hart acknowledged resume request
    // hart control
    output logic [19:0]                       hartsel_o,       // hartselect to ctrl module
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                haltreq_o,       // request to halt a hart
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumereq_o,     // request hart to resume

    input  logic                              cmderror_valid_i,  // an error occured
    input  dm::cmderr_t                       cmderror_i,        // this error occured
    input  logic                              cmdbusy_i,         // cmd is currently busy executing

    output logic [dm::ProgBufSize-1:0][31:0]  progbuf_o, // to system bus
    output logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_o,

    input  logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_i,
    input  logic                              data_valid_i,
    // system bus access module (SBA)
    output logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_o,

 * Description: Clock domain crossings for JTAG to DMI very heavily based

 *              This is mainly a wrapper around the existing CDCs.
 */
module dmi_cdc (
    // JTAG side (master side)
    input  logic             tck_i,
    input  logic             trst_ni,

    input  dm::dmi_req_t     jtag_dmi_req_i,
    output logic             jtag_dmi_ready_o,
    input  logic             jtag_dmi_valid_i,

    output dm::dmi_resp_t    jtag_dmi_resp_o,
    output logic             jtag_dmi_valid_o,
    input  logic             jtag_dmi_ready_i,

    // core side (slave side)
    input  logic             clk_i,
    input  logic             rst_ni,

    output dm::dmi_req_t     core_dmi_req_o,
    output logic             core_dmi_valid_o,
    input  logic             core_dmi_ready_i,

    input dm::dmi_resp_t     core_dmi_resp_i,
    output logic             core_dmi_ready_o,
    input  logic             core_dmi_valid_i
  );

  cdc_2phase #(.T(dm::dmi_req_t)) i_cdc_req (
    .src_rst_ni  ( trst_ni          ),
    .src_clk_i   ( tck_i            ),
    .src_data_i  ( jtag_dmi_req_i   ),
    .src_valid_i ( jtag_dmi_valid_i ),
    .src_ready_o ( jtag_dmi_ready_o ),

    .dst_rst_ni  ( rst_ni           ),
    .dst_clk_i   ( clk_i            ),
    .dst_data_o  ( core_dmi_req_o   ),
    .dst_valid_o ( core_dmi_valid_o ),
    .dst_ready_i ( core_dmi_ready_i )
  );

  cdc_2phase #(.T(dm::dmi_resp_t)) i_cdc_resp (
    .src_rst_ni  ( rst_ni           ),
    .src_clk_i   ( clk_i            ),
    .src_data_i  ( core_dmi_resp_i  ),
    .src_valid_i ( core_dmi_valid_i ),
    .src_ready_o ( core_dmi_ready_o ),

    .dst_rst_ni  ( trst_ni          ),
    .dst_clk_i   ( tck_i            ),
    .dst_data_o  ( jtag_dmi_resp_o  ),
    .dst_valid_o ( jtag_dmi_valid_o ),
    .dst_ready_i ( jtag_dmi_ready_i )
  );
endmodule

## O1 — DMI/JTAG Unauthenticated Access

**Evidence Summary:**

In `dmi_jtag.sv` lines 110-113, the DMI state machine gates **read** transactions on `pass_chk`, but **write** transactions have no authentication gate at all:

```systemverilog
// line 110-113 of dmi_jtag.sv
if ((dm::dtm_op_t'(dmi.op) == dm::DTM_READ) && (pass_chk == 1'b1)) begin
    state_d = Read;
end else if ((dm::dtm_op_t'(dmi.op) == dm::DTM_WRITE)) begin
    state_d = Write;    // <-- NO pass_chk check!
```

The `pass_chk` flag (line 79) is set only by `DTM_PASS` comparing `data_d == pass`, where `pass` is loaded from `jtag_key` at reset (lines 229-232). In both `ariane_xilinx.sv` (lines 218-235) and `serpent_peripherals.sv` (lines 105-122), the `jtag_key` port of `dmi_jtag` is **left unconnected**, meaning `pass` is undriven/zero. Even if connected, writes bypass authentication entirely.

Path: `JTAG pins (tck/tms/tdi/tdo)` → `dmi_jtag_tap` → `dmi_jtag` FSM → `dmi_cdc` → `dm_csrs`/`dm_top` → system bus / CSRs. No authentication gating on writes.

**Verdict:** `candidate_violation`

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| subject | DMI/JTAG requestor (dmi_jtag module via JTAG pins) |
| operation | Write DM CSRs (DMControl, SBA registers, etc.) via DTM_WRITE |
| object | DM CSRs (dmcontrol, sbaddress, sbdata), system bus via dm_top/dm_sba |
| expected_guard | Authentication check (pass_chk==1) before allowing DMI transactions |
| observed_behavior | DTM_WRITE transactions accepted unconditionally; pass_chk only gates DTM_READ; jtag_key unconnected in FPGA/OpenPiton top-levels |
| path | tck/tms/tdi → dmi_jtag_tap → dmi_jtag (state machine: Idle→Write) → dmi_cdc → dm_csrs/dm_top → system |
| state_condition | pass_chk=0 (or uninitialized); jtag_key floating; DTM_WRITE op=2'h2 always allowed at line 112 |
| impact | Unauthenticated debugger can set dmactive=1, ndmreset=1, program SBA for arbitrary system bus access, halt/resume CPU |
| rtl_evidence | dmi_jtag.sv:110-113 (write no gate); dmi_jtag.sv:114-120 (pass_chk only from DTM_PASS); ariane_xilinx.sv:218-235 (jtag_key unconnected); serpent_peripherals.sv:105-122 (jtag_key unconnected) |
